input.background: An urn contains one white, one black, one red, and one green sphere all of equal size. Three spheres are drawn at random. We want to determine the number of ways this can be done based on whether the order of selection is relevant or irrelevant.
input.question: In how many ways can three spheres be drawn if the order in which the choices are made is relevant?

output.answer: The number of permutations of three objects from a set of four objects (order is relevant) is $$P(4,3)=\frac{4!}{(4-3)!}=24$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
